event: The Virgin and Child with Saint Rosalina
symbols: roses offered to child — the crown of roses associated with Saint Rosalia and the rosary devotion
cherubim in clouds — the heavenly court attending the Virgin and Child
kneeling posture of saint — devotion and humility before the divine
female saint standing at right — a companion saint presenting or witnessing the devotion
blue mantle of Mary — her queenship and heavenly grace
description: The Virgin Mary, seated on clouds in a blue mantle, holds the Infant Jesus on her lap. A young woman in a brown religious habit kneels before them, offering or receiving roses from the Christ Child. A second female figure in white robes stands at the right, looking on. Above, two or three cherubim float in golden clouds. A faint landscape is visible in the lower left background. The palette is warm with golden light emanating from the upper register.
theological_description: This devotional composition presents the Virgin and Child receiving the veneration of saints, a common theme in Counter-Reformation art. The kneeling figure, identified as Saint Rosalina (or Rosalia of Palermo), offers roses — her traditional attribute — to the Christ Child, symbolizing the fragrance of sanctity and the beauty of a life consecrated to God. The scene reflects the Catholic theology of the communion of saints, where the blessed in heaven enjoy intimate access to Christ through the mediation of his mother. Mary's role as Mediatrix of graces is implied by her central position between the saints and the divine Child. The cherubim above place the encounter in a heavenly register, suggesting that what is depicted transcends historical time — it is an eternal communion. The theology of intercession is grounded in passages such as Hebrews 12:1 on the great cloud of witnesses and Revelation 5:8 on the prayers of the saints rising before the throne.

scripture_refs.reason: All generations will call me blessed — Mary's exalted role recognized by the saints who venerate her
The great cloud of witnesses surrounding the faithful
The prayers of the saints ascending like incense before the throne
I am a rose of Sharon — the rose symbolism associated with Christ and the saints

_meta.title: The Virgin and Child with St. Rosalina
